---
title: "Introduction to R"
author: "Yang Liu"
date: 12-20-2022
format:
  revealjs:
    slide-number: c/t
    width: 1600
    height: 900
    #logo: "https://www.uofmhealth.org/sites/default/files/UM-Health_Logo-Horizontal-White.png"
    #footer: "[R]"
    css: ["theme/theme.css"]
    theme: simple
    echo: true   #include codes and results
    incremental: true
    scrollable: true
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

## RStudio Cloud {background-image="images/cloud.png"}

[RStudio Cloud](https://posit.cloud/spaces/308660/content/5063771)

R, RStudio, data, codes for this workshop are preloaded

## Contents

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Structure

-   Basics
-   Data Management
-   Data Visualization
-   Data Summary
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Course datasets

-   covid_testing.xlsx

-   covid_result.xlsx

-   employee.dta
:::
:::::

## Basics

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Script editor

-   to type, run, and **save** your code

-   to display outcome

### Console

-   to type and run your code
-   to display outcome
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Environment

-   current datasets
-   variables

### Viewer

-   files: Intro R_student.qmd
-   packages
-   outcome (graph, table, slide)
:::
:::::

## Get ready

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Start a new project

-   Click right top corner
-   Start a new project (Folder)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Set working directory

-   Click Session
-   Set Working Directory
-   Choose Directory:/cloud/project
:::
:::::

## Install packages

install these packages once.

````         
``` {{r}}
#code chunk#1 
install.packages("tidyverse") #tidy data
install.packages("gtsummary") #create stats summary table
install.packages("labelled") #label variables
install.packages("skimr") #dataset summary
install.packages("haven") #import SPSS, Stata, SAS data
install.packages("readr") #import excel data
install.packages("qualtRics") #import Qualtrics data .csv
install.packages("Rcpp") #help to import data
```
````

## Load packages

tell R to load packages every session

```{r}
#code chunk#2
                 
library(tidyverse)
library(gtsummary)
library(labelled)
library(skimr)
library(haven)
library(readxl)
library(qualtRics)
```

## Main Packages

::::: columns
::: {.column width="40%"}
tidyverse --- Tidy data ![](images/tv.jfif){width="200"}
:::

::: {.column width="60%"}
ggplot2 --- Grammar of Graphics ![](images/gglogo.png){width="200"} gtsummary --- Grammar of Tables ![](images/gtlogo.png){width="200"}
:::
:::::

## Import data

Click File, Import Dataset, From Excel ...\
code chunk#3

```{r}
covid_testing <- read_excel("covid_testing.xlsx")
View(covid_testing)
```

## R programming

-   1+1 = 2 (human)

-   1+1 = x (human)

-   x \<- 1+1 (R)

-   object \<- input & argument (R)\
    code chunk#4

```{r}
#| output-location: fragment
x <- 1+1
x
```

-   Your turn: change 1+1 to 4+6, run\

## R programming

### object \<- function(x)

-   function --- does stuff

-   object --- stores output

-   example

-   covid_testing \<- read_excel("covid_testing.xlsx")

### function(x)

-   example
-   view(covid_testing)

## The pipe \|\> (%\>%)

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
x \|\>

function1 \|\>

function2 \|\>

function3
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
code chunk#5

```{r}
#| output-location: column-fragment
covid_testing |>
  print() |>
  dim() # dimensions(#observations, #variables) 
```
:::
:::::

## Inspect data

View dataset: click tab

columns - variables\
rows - observations\
code chunk#6

```{r}
#| output-location: column-fragment
glimpse(covid_testing)
```

## Inspect data

code chunk#7

```{r}
#| output-location: slide
skim(covid_testing)
```

## Recap

-   Start a project/set working directory

-   Install /load packages

-   Import data

-   Inspect data

## Data Management

Merge two datasets: covid_testing and covid_result

import covid_result\
code chunk#8

```{r}
#| output-location: fragment
covid_result <- read_excel("covid_result.xlsx")
View(covid_result)
dim(covid_result)
```

## Merge

code chunk#9

```{r}
#| output-location: fragment
covid_total <- full_join(covid_testing, covid_result, by="mrn")
view(covid_total)
dim(covid_total)
```

## Subset - select variables

Function: select

output \<- select(x, var1, var2, var3)\
code chunk#10 

```{r}
#| output-location: fragment
data1<- select(covid_total, mrn, age, result)
print(data1)
dim(data1)
```

## Subset - exclude variables

output \<- select(x, -var1, -var2, -var3)\
code chunk#11 

```{r}
#| output-location: fragment
data2<- select(covid_total, -mrn)
print(data2)
dim(data2)
```

## Subset - select observations

Function: filter

filter chooses observations that only match the condition.

Example: filter to get all covid testing valid results (positive or negative)\

output \<- filter(x, argument)\
code chunk#12 

```{r}
#| output-location: fragment
valid <- filter(covid_total, result != "invalid")
print(valid)
dim(valid)
```

## label variables

x \|\>

set_variable_labels(

var1 = "label1",

var2 = "label2"

)\
code chunk#13

```{r}
#| output-location: fragment
covid_total <- 
  covid_total |>
   set_variable_labels(
     pan_day = "Day of the pandemic",
     age = "Age")
var_label(covid_total)
```

## Recap

### Operators

-   \<-

-   \|\>

### Functions

-   full_join

-   select

-   filter

-   set_variable_lables\

## Data type

![](images/datatype.png){fig-align="center"}

## Visualize data - Histogram

Continuous - Histogram

Categorical - Bar graph

For histograms, the x-axis variable is continuous. Plot covid test counts during the first a few weeks of pandemic.\
code chunk#14

```{r}
#| output-location: fragment
covid_total |>
  ggplot(aes(x= pan_day)) +
  geom_histogram()
```

## Visualize data - Histogram

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#code chunk#15
covid_total |>
  ggplot(aes(x= pan_day)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth=1)
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#code chunk#16
covid_total |>
  ggplot(aes(x= pan_day, fill = result)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth=1)
```
:::
:::::

## Visualize data - Bar graph

For bar graphs, the x-axis variable is categorical

code chunk#17

```{r}
#| output-location: fragment
covid_total |>
   ggplot(aes(x = result)) +
   geom_bar()
```

## Visualize data - Bar graph

code chunk#18

```{r}
#| output-location: fragment
covid_total |>
   ggplot(aes(x = gender, fill = result)) +
   geom_bar()
```

## Visualize data - Scatter plot

Scatter plot of age vs time colored by gender, faceted by covid testing result

::: panel-tabset
## Code#19

```{r}
#| eval: false
covid_total |>
  filter(result != "invalid") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = pan_day, y = age, color = gender)) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_grid(~result)
```

## Plot

```{r}
#| echo: false
covid_total |>
  filter(result != "invalid") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = pan_day, y = age, color = gender)) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_grid(~result)
```
:::

## Recap

-   data visualization --- ggplot2

-   ggplot(aes(x= var, y=var, color=var)) +

    geom_function()

## Summary statistics

Create descriptive and summary tables

continuous variable: median(IQR)

categorical variable: n(%)\
code chunk#20

```{r}
#| output-location: slide
covid_total |>
  tbl_summary()
```

## Summary statistics

exclude mrn

exclude variables that need to be grouped\
code chunk#21

```{r}
#| output-location: slide
covid_total |>
  select(-mrn, -clinic_name) |>
  tbl_summary()
```

## Comparing group demographics

Describe demographics of covid positive group and covid negative group

code chunk#22

```{r}
#| output-location: slide
covid_total |>
  select(-mrn, -clinic_name) |>
  filter(result != "invalid") |>
  tbl_summary(
    by = result
  )
```

## Bivariate analysis

Covid positive vs covid negative

Question#1: if the proportion of gender is different?

Question#2: if the distribution of days of pandemic is different?

Question#3: if the distribution of age is different?\
code chunk#23

```{r}
#| output-location: slide
covid_total |> 
  select(-mrn, -clinic_name) |>
  filter(result != "invalid") |>
  tbl_summary(
    by = result
  ) |>
  add_p ()
```

## Your turn

create a summary table by gender\
dataset: covid_total\
variables: mrn, age, gender, clinic_name, result\
code chunk#24

````         
``` {{r}}
object |>
  function(-var, -var) |>
  function(
    by = var
 )
```
````

## Your turn (solution)

```{r}
#| output-location: slide
covid_total |>
  select(-mrn, -clinic_name) |>
  tbl_summary(
    by = gender
  )
```

## Alternative: Stata&R

Tidy data in Stata\
code chunk#25

```{r}
employee <- read_dta("employee.dta")
View(employee)
```

## Alternative: Stata&R

Create tables in R\
code chunk#26

```{r}
#| output-location: slide
employee |>
  select(-id) |>
  tbl_summary()
```

## Alternative: Stata&R

code chunk#27

```{r}
#| output-location: slide
employee |>
  select(-id) |>
  mutate_at("jobcat", as_factor) |>
  tbl_summary()
```

## Missing values

-   suppress printing count of unknown values\
    code chunk#28

```{r}
#| output-location: slide
employee |>
  select(-id) |>
  tbl_summary(
      missing ="no"
)  #n=474
```

## Missing values

-   remove all observations with missing values\
    code chunk#29

```{r}
#| output-location: slide
employee |>
  select(-id) |>
  drop_na() |>
  tbl_summary()  #n=473
```

## Recap

-   summary statistics --- gtsummary\
-   tbl_summary()

## Recap

-   Basics of R programming
-   Data management
-   Data Visualization
-   Data summary

## Main Packages

::::: columns
::: {.column width="40%"}
tidyverse --- Tidy data ![](images/tv.jfif){width="200"}
:::

::: {.column width="60%"}
ggplot2 --- Grammar of Graphics ![](images/gglogo.png){width="200"} gtsummary --- Grammar of Tables ![](images/gtlogo.png){width="200"}
:::
:::::

## Acknowledgement

<https://stephan-kadauke.quarto.pub/intro-to-r-for-clinical-data-rmed2022>

<https://www.danieldsjoberg.com/clinical-reporting-gtsummary-rmed>/

## Thank you for taking the time to learn R

-   Install R and RStudio on your computer
-   Install R packages
-   Learning by Doing
-   Codebook: Intro R_student.qmd

## Self-teaching website

https://bookdown.org/pdr_higgins/rmrwr/

## Install R and RStudio

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
### For Windows users

[Download R for Windows](https://cran.rstudio.com/bin/windows/base/R-4.5.2-win.exe)

[Download RStudio for Windows](https://download1.rstudio.org/electron/windows/RStudio-2025.09.2-418.exe)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### For macOS users

[Download R for macOS 11 and higher](https://cran.rstudio.com/bin/macosx/big-sur-arm64/base/R-4.5.2-arm64.pkg)

[Download RStudio for macOS](https://download1.rstudio.org/electron/macos/RStudio-2025.09.2-418.dmg)
:::
:::::

## Support

-   Email: ylyl\@med.umich.edu anytime
-   Schedule a 30-min meeting (two-week)
-   Request a 60-min workshop (four-week)
